title: cerastium viscidum var. kotschyi
intro: Cerastium viscidum var. kotschyi, also known as the sticky mouse-ear chickweed, is a plant species within the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. The medicinal properties of Cerastium viscidum var. kotschyi include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote the healing process of wounds. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, which are applied topically to affected areas to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
